result:
Done. All deliverables are produced, dual-verified, and consistent.

## Result

**Headline replicates.** Base sample (N=64): 2SLS coefficient of `logpgp95` on `avexpr` is **0.94** (SE 0.16) — nearly double the OLS **0.52** (SE 0.06) — on a **strong first stage** (β on `logem4` = −0.61, correctly signed; **F = 22.9**). This is AJR's headline, including the 2SLS ≫ OLS feature the manuscript leans on.

| Specification | N | OLS (SE) | 2SLS (SE) | 1st-stage β̂(logem4) | 1st-stage F | Identification |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Base (bivariate) | 64 | 0.52 (0.06) | **0.94 (0.16)** | −0.61 | **22.9** | strong |
| + latitude | 64 | 0.47 (0.06) | 1.00 (0.22) | −0.51 | 13.1 | strong |
| + continents (africa, asia) | 64 | 0.43 (0.05) | 0.84 (0.19) | −0.53 | 11.0 | strong (marginal) |
| Drop neo-Europes | 60 | 0.49 (0.08) | 1.28 (0.36) | −0.39 | 8.6 | ⚠️ **WEAK** |
| Africa only | 27 | 0.30 (0.11) | 2.40 (3.99) | −0.11 | 0.3 | ⚠️ **COLLAPSED** |

**What survives:** both control perturbations (latitude, continents) — 2SLS stays in 0.84–1.00, first stage above the F≈10 threshold. **What does not:** both *sample* perturbations lose identification. Dropping the neo-Europes pulls F to 8.6; Africa-only collapses it to 0.3 (instrument coefficient statistically zero, CI [−5.4, 10.2]). The right reading is **symmetric** — a collapsed first stage neither confirms nor refutes the coefficient, it can't identify it. The manuscript may claim replication + robustness to controls on a strong first stage; it may **not** present the weak-spec 2SLS points as reliable, nor read their larger values as a bigger effect.

## Orchestration (as planned)
- **Analysis + all deliverables:** me, inline (Opus lead) — compact, deterministic, reasoning-heavy work kept with the lead. 2SLS via **`AER::ivreg`** (correct IV SEs), first-stage F = partial F on the excluded instrument via `car::linearHypothesis`.
- **1 delegation** (of ≤3): **blind cross-vendor replication** via Codex, correctly pinned to **gpt-5.6-terra · xhigh**, in an isolated dir with no access to my files. It wrote its own R and **agreed to reported precision on all five specs**. It also independently hit — and fixed — the same environmental gotcha I did (`colonial` ships as a `data.table`, breaking `[, cols]` indexing), a clean signal the two lines were genuinely independent. Bonus: `results.csv` and the figure came back **byte-identical** to the prior run's SHA256.
- No Workflow / deep-reasoner / high-stakes dual — the task is below the threshold where fan-out adds leverage and is directly verifiable by running it.

## Deliverables (in `runs/opus/high-ajr/`)
`script.R` · `robustness-table.md` · `memo.md` (456 words) · plus `results.csv` (full precision), `robustness-figure.png` (Okabe-Ito, 2000×1300 @ 300 dpi, no in-plot title), refreshed `run-log.md` and `SHA256SUMS`. Script runs clean (exit 0, no warnings).
